name: Hoya Method (4x4)
description: An alternative 4x4 speedsolving method developed by Jong-Ho Jeong (known as Hoya). Unlike Yau, Hoya solves opposite centers first, then all four cross edges, then the remaining centers, before proceeding to edge pairing and a standard 3x3 finish. This order makes center building easier since cross edges are already placed, and offers a flexible, intuitive approach to 4x4 solving.

steps.name: Opposite Centers
steps.description: Solve two opposite centers (typically white and yellow). Starting with just two centers gives maximum freedom for piece placement and efficient commutators.
tips: Pick the two easiest opposite centers to start
Use wide moves freely since nothing else is solved
Plan the first center in inspection

steps.name: All Cross Edges
steps.description: Pair and place all 4 cross dedges (double-edges) into their correct positions relative to the solved centers. Having only two centers solved provides enough freedom to pair edges efficiently.
tips: Pair edges using slice moves without disrupting centers
Plan multiple edge pairs in advance
Use the unsolved sides to stage edge pieces

steps.name: Remaining Centers
steps.description: Complete the remaining 4 centers around the already-placed cross edges. This step requires careful commutators to avoid disrupting the cross.
tips: Solve adjacent centers in pairs for efficiency
Use commutator-style insertions to preserve cross edges
This step is easier because the cross constrains piece positions

steps.name: 3-2-3 Edge Pairing
steps.description: Pair all remaining dedges using the 3-2-3 technique, the same efficient edge pairing approach used in the Yau method. Place 3 edges in a slice, pair them, then handle 2 and 3 more.
tips: Same 3-2-3 technique used in Yau applies here
Look ahead to the next batch while pairing
Minimize unnecessary slice moves and regrips

steps.name: F2L (First Two Layers)
steps.description: With the cube reduced to a 3x3 state and the cross already complete, solve the first two layers by inserting corner-edge pairs into their slots using standard CFOP techniques.
tips: Standard CFOP F2L applies directly
Cross is already solved, so begin F2L immediately
Look ahead to OLL during last F2L pair

steps.name: OLL (Orientation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Orient all pieces on the last layer so the top face is one color. OLL parity may need to be resolved on 4x4.
tips: Recognize OLL parity before starting the algorithm
Standard 3x3 OLL algorithms apply when no parity
OLL parity adds approximately 12 moves

steps.name: PLL (Permutation of Last Layer)
steps.description: Permute all last layer pieces into their correct positions to complete the solve. PLL parity may also occur.
tips: Learn a fast PLL parity algorithm
PLL parity occurs roughly half the time
Standard 3x3 PLL applies when no parity

pros: Centers are easier to build after cross edges are placed
Shares many techniques with Yau, making it easy to switch
cons: Less popular than Yau, so fewer tutorials and resources
Slightly less efficient cross building than Yau
Edge pairing step is identical but center step can be awkward
creator: Jong-Ho Jeong (Hoya)
